title: casparia pansamalana
intro: Casparia pansamalana is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae, commonly known as the Philippine verbena or pan-asal. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Casparia pansamalana include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-spasmodic agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
